sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Looking at the image, the right green square appears to have moved! It's now closer to the right frame. Let me verify with the segmentation.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current positions
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        b = n['boundary']
        center = (sum(p[0] for p in b)//len(b), sum(p[1] for p in b)//len(b))
        print(f"  green: center={center}")

# Compare with previous
prev_seg = previous_frame.segmentation
for n in prev_seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        b = n['boundary']
        center = (sum(p[0] for p in b)//len(b), sum(p[1] for p in b)//len(b))
        print(f"  prev green: center={center}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL RESULT: python
sections.content:
green: center=(28, 19)
  green: center=(31, 43)
  prev green: center=(28, 19)
  prev green: center=(31, 40)

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Excellent! So after the MOUSE click on the right green and then RIGHT:
- Left green: (28, 19) - unchanged
- Right green: (31, 43) - moved from (31, 40) to (31, 43)
So the MOUSE click on the right green square made it the controlled one, and then RIGHT moved it! This is a key discovery.
So the control scheme is:
- MOUSE click on a green square selects it as the controlled one.
- UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT moves the selected green square.
Now I need to move the right green from (31, 43) to (28, 46). That's UP 1 and RIGHT 1 (or UP 1 and RIGHT 1, since 43 + 3 = 46).
Wait, let me check: the right green moved from (31, 40) to (31, 43), which is 3 columns to the right. So each RIGHT moves the green by 3 columns. Similarly, each UP moves the green by 3 rows up.
So to move the right green from (31, 43) to (28, 46):
- UP: (31, 43) -> (28, 43)
- RIGHT: (28, 43) -> (28, 46)
Let me try this sequence.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Right green at (31, 43), need to move to (28, 46)
# UP: (31, 43) -> (28, 43)
# RIGHT: (28, 43) -> (28, 46)
action(['UP', 'RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
